text: Gender transformation in the Circle of Concerned African Women Theologians through Mainstreaming Gender in Higher Theological Education in Africa The study aims to highlight the gender transformation through mainstreaming gender in higher theological education achieved by the Circle of Concerned African Women Theologians. Taking the framework of African Women’s Theology into account, the study examined efforts the Circle has undertaken toward realisation of gender equity, equality, and solidarity within the context of education generally and higher theological education in particular, while mentoring young African women for an equal role as scholars within academia. This study explored the impact of the Circle of Concerned African Women Theologians on mainstreaming gender in higher theological education through the African Women’s Theology prism and its struggle in eliminating men from the theological institution at Africa in a qualitative study research approach.
